<!--
IMPORTANT: This template is for security researchers and community members.

DO NOT use this template for non-security issues.
For general bugs, use the standard issue template.

PLEASE report security issues privately using one of these methods:
1. GitHub Private Security Advisory (preferred): https://github.com/Claudient/Claudient/security/advisories/new
2. Email: security@claudient.dev
3. Email fallback: security@uitbreiden.com

## Vulnerability Type
<!-- Select one: Code injection | Credential exposure | Privilege escalation | Supply chain | Other (describe) -->

## Severity
<!-- Critical / High / Medium / Low / Informational -->

## Affected Component
<!-- e.g., "skill: fastapi-crud.md", "hook: pre-commit-security.sh", "agent: security-reviewer.md", "MCP: anthropic-tools" -->

## Affected Versions
<!-- e.g., "v1.2.0 and below" or "all versions including v1.5.1" -->

## Summary
<!-- Brief, non-specific description -->

## How to Report
Instead of detailing the vulnerability here, please report it privately using one of these methods:

### Method 1: GitHub Private Security Advisory (Recommended)
Go to: https://github.com/Claudient/Claudient/security/advisories/new

Include:
- Vulnerability description
- Affected versions
- Reproduction steps (minimal)
- Suggested fix (optional)
- CVSS score or severity assessment

### Method 2: Email
Email: `security@claudient.dev` or `security@uitbreiden.com`

Subject: `[SECURITY] Vulnerability Report: <brief title>`

Include the same information as the advisory form.

## Embargo Agreement
Our typical embargo period is 30 days from patch release for high/critical vulnerabilities. We will:
- Acknowledge your report within 24 hours
- Provide regular status updates
- Publish a public advisory after the patch is released
- Credit you in the advisory (unless you request anonymity)

Thank you for helping keep Claudient secure!
